definitions: To leave a place or situation, often abruptly or in protest.
To get rid of something, especially an illness or an unwanted feeling, by walking.
To steal something by simply walking away with it.
To complete a task or win a game with ease.
examples: She decided to walk off the job after the argument.
He tried to walk off his headache with a long stroll.
Someone tried to walk off with my backpack.
They managed to walk off with the championship trophy.
